Request:

while  reducing PY 2023 Comprehensive Planning from $35,000 to $26,500.  
  Funding:

Request:  
Requesting authorization of professional services contract with Thornton, Musso, Bellemin , Inc. 
in the amount of $17,127.20 for the sampling and analysis of our filter media at Ed Love WTP.  
 Project Description:  
Ed Love has several water filters that contain different layers of media to remove particles from 
water before the water moves on to other treatment steps. Over time, the media loses its  
functionality and needs replacing. With this evaluation, TMB will analyze  the media to 
determine if it needs cleaning or replacing.

This project will take approximately 6 weeks to complete.

Funded by Ed Love 60109045 -3100.

  General Information  
 The Municipal Water Pollution Prevention Report (MWPP) is a required annual submission to ADEM. The MWPP summarizes the condition of the entire sanitary sewer system during the prior calendar year and is due annually by May 31st.  Each sanitary sewer system permitted by ADEM receives a point total between 0 and 783 points.  Lower point totals are preferable.   
 The Hilliard N Fletcher WRRF received a total score of 1 40 points for calendar year 202 5.

 Re: ALDOT Hwy 69 and Skyland Blvd Grade Project  Utility Relocations  
 WS Project 2026. 715.001 
 ALDOT Engineering Services  Agreement  
  
 
Request:  
Requesting authorization for the mayor to execute a n agreement for engineering services between  
Hazen and Sawyer  and the City of Tuscaloosa for the ALDOT Hwy 69 and Skyland Blvd Utility Relocations 
project in the amount of $48,748.44 .  
 
Project Description: 
The limits of the ALDOT project are approximately from Mimosa Park Road to north of the I- 59 Overpass 
along Hwy 69 South. This also includes a section of Skyland Blvd from the intersection of Hwy 69 South to Old Greensboro Road. The general scope of the project includes grading, drainage, base and paving, 
lighting, signals, and ITS.  
 The City of Tuscaloosa will be required to relocate water and sanitary sewer utilities to accommodate the ALDOT project.  
 Project Funding: 
The full cost, $270,308.44,  of utility relocation engineering and construction is reimbursable under  this 
SAHD No. 2 Utility Agreement between ALDOT and the City of Tuscaloosa.

MEMORANDUM  
 
To: Finance Committee  
CC: Carly Standridge , CFO ; Brent Blankley, Executive Director of Public Safety ; and Acting Chief 
of Police Severn Sanders  
Date:  March 27 , 202 6 
Subject:  FY25 Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant (JAG) Program 
 
Accounting & Finance  and Tuscaloosa Police Department  are seeking approval to submit an 
application to the United States  Department of Justice’s Office of Justice Programs for the FY2 5 
Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant (JAG) . 
 
Scope  
The Edward Byrne Memorial Justice Assistance Grant is an annual formula allocation shared 
between the City of Tuscaloosa and Tuscaloosa County Commission. Requests for the utilization 
of FY25 funds include a patrol vehicle for Tuscaloosa Police Department and MDUs for the 
Tuscaloosa County Sheriff's Office.  
 Funding  (no City  match)  
Federal  request  $ 81,073.00  
City’s portion  $63,610.00  
County’s portion  $17,463.00  
 
 
3
 
 
MEMORANDUM  
 
To: Finance Committee  
CC: Carly Standridge , CFO ; Brent Blankley, Executive Director of Public Safety ; and Chief of Fire 
and Rescue  Mark Delk  
Date:  March 27 , 202 6 
Subject:  Safety First Grant  
 
Accounting & Finance  and Tuscaloosa Fire and Rescue  are seeking approval to submit an 
application to Norfolk Southern  for the Safety First Grant.  
 Scope  
The Safety First Grant is intended to support organizations that directly provide safety services 
or operate programs that promote safe behavior. If awarded, Tuscaloosa Fire and Rescue 
intend to use the proceeds to pay for hazmat equipment.  
 Funding  (no City  match)  
Grantor  request  $ 15,000.00  
 
 
4
 
 
MEMORANDUM  
 
To: Finance Committee  
CC: Carly Standridge , CFO ; Director of Arts & Entertainment, Kay Day  
Date:  March 27 , 202 6 
Subject:  America250AL Semi Quincentennial Community Grants  
 
Accounting & Finance  and Arts & Entertainment  are seeking approval to submit an application to 
America250AL to request funding for our community celebration, Celebration on the River.  
 Scope  
The America250AL  Grant will support locally driven commemorations of the 250th anniversary 
of the signing of the Declaration of Independence  such as Celebration on the River.  
 
Funding  (no City  match)  
State  request  $2,500.00
